channel.title: healthTest
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from healthTest, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: Trump administration lauds plastic surgeons' statement on trans surgery for minors
item.description: A patient who came to regret the top surgery she got as a teen won a $2 million malpractice suit. Then, the American Society of Plastic Surgeons clari...

item.title: Glyphosate: What Indigenous communities have suspected for years about the dangers of the herbicide
item.description: There have been renewed questions around the safety of the herbicide glyphosate in light of the recent retraction of an influential peer-reviewed rese...

item.title: Choosing an IVF embryo is uncertain: A new dish design could improve what labs see
item.description: Selecting the healthiest embryo is one of the most important steps in inâvitro fertilization (IVF), yet it remains one of the most uncertain. Roughly ...

item.title: Neglected killer: kala-azar disease surges in Kenya
item.description: For nearly a year, repeated misdiagnoses of the deadly kala-azar disease left 60-year-old Harada Hussein Abdirahman's health deteriorating, as an outb...

item.title: 'House burping': What is this German habit and is it good for your health?
item.description: "House burping" is the latest thing cluttering people's feeds: short clips of people flinging open every window and door, announcing they're "burping"...

item.title: Why Huntington's proteins pile up: Two key tags guide their disposal
item.description: There is no known cure for Huntington's disease. A genetic mutation creates harmful proteins that accumulate and cause the disease's typical symptoms....

item.title: Weaponizing food allergies in entertainment endangers lives [PODCAST]
item.description: Subscribe to The Podcast by KevinMD. Watch on YouTube. Catch up on old episodes! Leading advocate for airline safety measures to protect food-allergic...

item.title: This season, 'The Pitt' is about what doesn't happen in one day
item.description: The first season of The Pitt was about acute problems. The second is about chronic ones.

item.title: RFK Jr. speaks about beefing up red meat consumption at Cattle Con
item.description: Beef producers from around the country held a convention this week where they heard from Secretary of Health and Human Services Robert F. Kennedy Jr.,...

item.title: An ultrasound test may more reliably detect ovarian cancer in premenopausal women than the Risk of Malignancy Index
item.description: 1. This cohort study found that the IOTA ADNEX ultrasound model at a 10% risk threshold detected more ovarian cancers in premenopausal women than the ...

item.title: They're cured of leprosy. Why do they still live in leprosy colonies?
item.description: Leprosy is one of the least contagious diseases around â and perhaps one of the most misunderstood. The colonies are relics of a not-too-distant past ...

item.title: Geriatric diabetes management: Why strict A1c targets can harm seniors
item.description: According to the CDC, nearly 1 in 3 Americans over age 65 live with diabetes. However, our health care system treats diabetes as if all adults are met...

item.title: A physicianâs quiet reflection on January 1, 2026
item.description: Over the past few days, I have read numerous articles reflecting on 2025: what people achieved, what they lost, how many books they read, and how they...

item.title: Why progression independent of relapse activity is the silent driver of disability in multiple sclerosis
item.description: For too long, multiple sclerosis (MS) has been described with an air of quiet fatalism, a chronic, debilitating disease managed primarily by reducing ...

item.title: Why medical school DEI mission statements matter for future physicians
item.description: When you were applying to medical school, how did you decide which programs to consider? After filtering by stats and geography, you likely browsed sc...

item.title: Neonatal Hyperbilirubinemia and navigating the 2022 AAP guideline updates
item.description: Key Points: The 2022 American Academy of Pediatrics guidelines slightly raised phototherapy thresholds for most infants to balance preventing encephal...

item.title: California's largest children's hospital system ends gender-affirming care for youth
item.description: Two hospitals in California are discontinuing hormone treatments for transgender youth, citing Trump administration pressures. In the past year, many ...

item.title: Knee bracing may provide small improvements in patient-reported outcomes in knee osteoarthritis
item.description: 1. Adding compartment-specific knee bracing with an adherence support intervention to standard advice, information and exercise (AIE) provides a small...

item.title: Chimeric antigen receptor T-cell therapy may induce sustained remission in multirefractory autoimmune hemolytic anemia

item.title: Comedy on Prescription? Pilot Raises Hopes for NHS Care
item.description: Early pilot data suggest comedy-based social prescribing could complement care, boost wellbeing, and ease pressure on NHS services. Press Association

item.title: Home Visits Don't Reduce Heat-Related ED Use in Older Adults
item.description: Medicaid home visits before heat events are not associated with changes in emergency department visits among older adults during extreme heat, a study...

item.title: Which Imaging Method Best Detects At-Risk MASH?
item.description: Noninvasive imaging tests may help identify at-risk metabolic dysfunction-associated steatohepatitis, with a FibroScan-based scoring system performing...

item.title: Study Maps Extensive Disease Links Beyond Childhood Eczema
item.description: Using millions of NHS records, researchers mapped extensive links between childhood eczema and later inflammatory and eye diseases. Medscape News UK

item.title: FDA Updates Risks in Capecitabine, Fluorouracil Labels
item.description: The labeling updates address the risk of early-onset toxicity, including life-threatening adverse events in patients with DPD deficiency who are treat...

item.title: Second-stage cesarean and operative vaginal deliveries are associated with similar neurodevelopmental outcomes in children
